steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the value of $y$ when $x = 0$. Substitute: $y = 400(3)^{2(0)} = 400(1) = 400$. Since $x$ counts six-month periods starting from the end of January 1997, $x = 0$ is that starting moment. The $y$-intercept means 400 subscribers at the end of January 1997.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six-month period was 400.": that's $x = 1$, not $x = 0$. ✗
  • "The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1997 was 400.": matches the $y$-intercept exactly. ✓
  • "The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1998 was 1,200.": the $y$-intercept is a starting value, not a rate. ✗
  • "The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1997 was 1,200.": that's what $x$ represents, not $y$. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
